gpt_review.biasedText: GET TODAY NEWSMAX+: NEWSMAX is the fastest-growing cable news channel in America with more than 30 million people watching! Reuters Institute reports NEWSMAX is one of the top news brands in the U.S. You need to watch NEWSMAX today. Get it with great shows from Rob Schmitt, Greta Van Susteren, Greg Kelly, Carl Higbie, Rob Finnerty – and many more! Find the NEWSMAX channel on your cable system – Go Here Now BEST OFFER: Sign up for NEWSMAX+ and get NEWSMAX, our streaming channel NEWSMAX2 and our military channel World at War. Find hundreds of shows, movies and specials. Even get Jon Voight's special series and President Trump's comedy programs and much more! Watch NEWSMAX+ on your smartphone or home TV app. Watch NEWSMAX anytime, anywhere! Start your FREE trial now: NewsmaxPlus.com
gpt_review.reason: I flagged similar text as Sensationalism/Emotionalism. The detection of promotional content blurring news and advertising is valid, though labeling it 'Elite vs. Populist Bias' is a stretch — it's more accurately self-promotional sensationalism than elite/populist framing. Still, the appeal to popularity and authority is a defensible reading.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: The article interviews only one polling analyst who downplays the impact of the strikes on Republican voters and frames the divergence in polls as a methodological issue.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: The entire article relies on a single source — Patrick Allocco — who consistently minimizes political risk for Trump and Republicans. No opposing analysts or critical voices are included. Sonnet completely missed this significant omission bias.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: A Reuters survey released Monday found only 27% of American adults approved of the strikes. An InsiderAdvantage poll, released Sunday, showed 54% approval.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: The article presents polling data but frames the divergence exclusively through Allocco's reassuring lens, de-emphasizing the low approval figures and the CNN finding that 23% of Republicans disapproved. Sonnet missed this structural slant in how the information is presented.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: NEWSMAX is the fastest-growing cable news channel in America with more than 30 million people watching!
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: The promotional material at the end uses hyperbolic, sensationalized language to self-promote, blurring news content and advertising. Sonnet flagged nothing at all in this article.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: The article interviews only one polling analyst who downplays the impact of the strikes on Republican voters and frames the divergence in polls as a methodological issue.
gpt_false_negatives.reason: The entire article relies on a single source who consistently minimizes political risk for Trump. No opposing analysts, critics, or alternative interpretations of the polling data are presented. GPT missed this significant omission bias.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: A Reuters survey released Monday found only 27% of American adults approved of the strikes. An InsiderAdvantage poll, released Sunday, showed 54% approval.
gpt_false_negatives.reason: The article presents divergent polling data but immediately channels interpretation through Allocco's reassuring framing, structurally de-emphasizing low approval figures and Republican disapproval. GPT missed this slant in how information is organized and contextualized.
